response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with deep experience in web, full-stack, and systems programming, but their profile diverges significantly from this role's core requirement of current, hands-on C++ expertise for industrial automation. Their C++ experience is limited to an early-career role (NI LabWindows/CVI) and a university thesis, with the past decade spent primarily in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. They lack exposure to IEC 62443, SDL practices, MQTT, and industrial protocols, all central to this position. While DLang low-latency and event-driven work shows transferable systems skills, the gap in modern C++ and industrial cybersecurity is too large for a senior role expected to mentor others on these exact standards.
strengths: Broad systems experience including low-latency, asynchronous I/O services handling millions of requests per second
Event-driven architecture experience via an open-source geo-data platform and microservices work
Strong engineering culture fit: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: C++ experience is dated (early-career role); C++ is absent from their current skills list
No evidence of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, MQTT, or industrial automation protocol experience
Recent career trajectory is web/full-stack and AI tooling, not high-performance C++ or embedded industrial systems

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent C++ / STL expertise
